title: set_subscription accepts arbitrary string for expires_at without format validation
description: store.rs:set_subscription() is the only write path to subscription expiry (CLAUDE.md invariant #12). It accepts expires_at: &str with no validation. The SQLite datetime invariant (CLAUDE.md item 9) requires format 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS' for comparisons with datetime('now') to work. An RFC 3339 string ('2026-04-16T12:00:00Z') stored here would silently make subscription_expiry() always return None (the comparison expires_at > datetime('now') would fail because 'T' sorts differently). The Phase 3 payment watcher must know the exact format requirement, but there is no guard in the code. A format mismatch produces no error -- just silently broken subscription gating. Fix: validate or enforce the format in set_subscription(), or accept a chrono::DateTime<Utc> and format it correctly internally.
